assumptions: The developer has a supported browser, email address, and a local application workspace.
No Descope account, project, Flow, or SDK integration exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a Descope account and project, embed a Descope authentication experience in an application, and complete a test sign-up or sign-in that yields an authenticated user and session.

first_success.outcome: A test sign-up or sign-in through the selected Descope Flow or SDK path produces an authenticated user and session.
first_success.observable_signal: The React quickstart's Application is working checkpoint, or an equivalent onSuccess/session authenticated state for the selected framework route.

route_selection.surface: Descope Getting Started Quickstart and framework Flow guides
route_selection.rule: Select Native versus Hosted Flows and one framework route only after those peer choices are fixed; Flows are recommended but not a single fully specified universal path.

candidates.name: Native Implementation with Descope Flows in React
candidates.reason: Flows are the recommended easiest approach and React has a concrete quickstart, but Native versus Hosted and many framework peers remain unranked for a universal first-mile path.

candidates.name: Hosted Application with Descope Flows
candidates.reason: Official getting started presents Hosted Application as a peer Flow-based option beside Native Implementation.

candidates.name: Client SDKs without Flows
candidates.reason: Documented as an alternative when not using Flows; the guide recommends Flows as easiest.

candidates.name: Backend SDKs without Flows
candidates.reason: Requires owning frontend and session behavior; not the recommended easiest route.

required_path.action: Sign up for Descope and obtain a project with a Project ID.

required_fields.label: Console signup and project creation fields
required_fields.notes: Docs say a Project ID is required to start, but the public Console shell does not document signup, verification, or onboarding fields.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can copy a Project ID from project settings.

required_path.action: Confirm the project contains the sign-up-or-in Flow used by the selected quickstart.

required_fields.label: Flow ID
required_fields.notes: Getting started centers on Flows; exact default Flow presence for a fresh project is not independently observed here.

required_path.observable_result: A usable Flow ID is available for SDK embedding.

required_path.action: Install the selected framework SDK, wrap the app with AuthProvider, and render the Descope Flow component.

required_fields.label: Framework-specific provider and Flow component wiring
required_fields.notes: React is one of many framework routes; Native versus Hosted changes the required implementation surface.

required_path.observable_result: The local app renders a Descope authentication experience.

required_path.action: Complete a test sign-up or sign-in and confirm authenticated user and session state.

required_fields.label: Flow-defined user inputs
required_fields.notes: Required inputs depend on the Flow's screens and authentication methods.

required_path.observable_result: onSuccess or session hooks report an authenticated user and the quickstart checkpoint can be marked complete.

prerequisites.description: A Descope account and project are required before a Project ID and Flow can be used.

external_gates.description: Flow authentication methods may require email delivery or third-party identity-provider authorization.

platform_outcomes.description: Descope issues a session token at the end of a successful Flow.

excluded.item: Reading getting-started documentation as a measured action
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is evidence, not a product action.

excluded.item: Backend session validation and Flow customization after first success
excluded.reason: The React quickstart places backend session management and customization after Application is working.

sources.title: React Quickstart
evidence_supported: Application is working style first-success framing

sources.title: Descope Console
evidence_supported: Console entry URL; signup field set not publicly documented

uncertainties.question: Which Flow delivery mode and framework define the selected Descope first-success route: Native versus Hosted, and which SDK quickstart?
uncertainties.impact: Peer choices change install packages, hosted redirect behavior, required code, and verification steps.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Fix one bounded framework and Flow delivery mode, then verify the complete current Console-to-app path.

uncertainties.question: What exact Descope Console signup, verification, onboarding wizard, and first-project fields are required today?
uncertainties.impact: Account-creation actions and field counts cannot be verified, so the audit cannot start cleanly at account creation.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated screenshots or a read-only walkthrough from public signup through Project ID visibility.

uncertainties.question: Does every fresh project include a usable sign-up-or-in Flow without additional Console editing?
uncertainties.impact: Missing or differently named default Flows would add required Console configuration before the SDK path works.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Fresh-project observation of default Flows and the Flow ID expected by the selected quickstart.
